title: AssetRef
description: The runtime object representing a resolved asset. Returned by host.assets.get(), host.assets.pick(), and stored in saved tool state. Uniform across library assets and user uploads — tools handle them identically.

source.description: 'library' = global catalog; 'user' = uploaded by this user; 'remote' = fetched by URL (rare, network capability only).

id.description: For library: the canonical asset id (e.g. 'suse/logo/primary'). For user: a host-generated local id. For remote: the source URL.

url.description: An object URL or data URL the template can use directly in , , etc. The host manages its lifecycle.

version.description: For library assets: the version resolved. Stored in saved state so re-opens get the same bytes (with fallback to latest if that version is gone).

meta.description: Type-specific metadata. e.g. for palettes: { swatches: [...] }.
